enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a life update from Boulder in 2015, describing his obsessive work on a Google Earth mapping project of Native American tribal/linguistic groups. He shares detailed plans for future bike trips along the Colorado Trail and Great Divide Trail, muses on comparing bike travel speeds to historical horse travel, and discusses selling his van to get out of debt.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Most fascinating project he's ever done; wants to map all Native American tribes and linguistic groups worldwide
Bikes outperform horses on virtually all terrain over a full day; even a barefoot human might beat a horse in an endurance race
Won't do the Colorado Trail until financially secure, won't do the Divide Trail until out of debt
Needs to sell it — too many repair costs for a vehicle he barely uses
Ancient trade route tourism (including in Iraq/Middle East) could replace dying physical-interaction industries and reduce conflict
Can't ride while deep in the mapping project because he'd only be thinking about the website

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I just got really high and realized I didn't do a video for a while.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's really warm outside now, like summer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got a haircut.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been hammering on my website and started two new pages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The new page I started yesterday is the most fascinating one I've ever done in my entire life.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The page before that was the second most fascinating.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't been writing on my bike because my bike computer wasn't working.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had to order a new part for my bike computer because they changed the technology.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My hub is too thick around for the new bike computer part to attach.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to call the bike computer company today to figure out what's going on.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't think I want to ride my bike anyway because the program I'm doing on my website requires a steady hand and concentration.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been wanting to mark out areas with different colors on Google Earth that you could click on and off with visuals like layers.

claims.subject: Google Earth
claims.claim: Google Earth never had the feature to mark out areas with different colors that you could click on and off with visuals like layers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I noticed Google Earth Pro is newly free and tried to download it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working on mapping Native American tribes and linguistic groups on Google Earth.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I found a Native American tribe map that's like 3,000 pixels by 4,000 pixels and 8 megabytes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I completed mapping Italy with Roman provinces or states and the tribes around it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The amazing thing with Google Earth is you can control the opacity and see the different sizes of tribes superimposed on the map.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got started on this project because I was working on my bike racer level page and wanted to map trails.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I came up with the idea to show trails at different area sizes from 10 meters up to 2,580 kilometers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The average horse travels about 30 miles a day over hilly and flat terrain.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: A bike can travel faster than a horse over any terrain, even hard terrain.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: A human without a bike could probably beat a horse in an endurance race over a day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The fastest record for the Continental Divide Trail is 17 days, averaging 130 miles a day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to memorize the whole area of America between Banff and New Mexico by riding the trail multiple times.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I see the future of social interaction and economies in tourism and traveling ancient routes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My next outdoor excursions are to mountain bike around Boulder and possibly the Colorado Trail in 2016.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to sell my van because it's costing me money to maintain and I don't drive it much.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to make money from my website and videos to support my biking and traveling lifestyle.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to do the Colorado Trail in 2016 and the Divide Trail in 2017.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to do the Divide Trail for 13 years because I like the number 13.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to figure out how to do screen capture recordings on my Mac for my Google Earth videos.
